perturbation.original_text: Dr. Murray will receive an annual royalty on net sales (defined as gross sales minus returns) for any products (the "Dr. Murray Products") developed by Dr. Murray for EHN for as long as the Dr. Murray Products are being sold.
perturbation.changed_text: Dr. Murray will receive an annual royalty on net sales (defined as gross sales, *including* returns) for any products (the "Dr. Murray Products") developed by Dr. Murray for EHN for as long as the Dr. Murray Products are being sold.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'net sales' from 'gross sales minus returns' to 'gross sales including returns' introduces a direct contradiction within the royalty calculation clause. This would inflate the net sales and lead to disagreements on the royalties owed, especially when considering Section 5.3 which clearly specifies the royalty based on a scale calculated after subtracting returns.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Royalty/Commission Payments. Dr. Murray will receive an annual royalty on net sales (defined as gross sales minus returns) for any products (the "Dr. Murray Products") developed by Dr. Murray for EHN for as long as the Dr. Murray Products are being sold. The Dr. Murray Products will be listed on Schedule A attached hereto as they are developed and added to product portfolio. During each year of this agreement, Dr. Murray will be paid no later than the 30st day of January based on the cumulative Net Sales of the Dr. Murray Products for the preceding 12 months based upon the following scale: Net Sales per 12 Months Royalty $2,000,001 to $3,500,000 $150,000 $3,500,001 to $5,000,000 $250,000 $5,000,001 to $10,000,000 $350,000 $10,000,001 to $25,000,000 $500,000 $25,000,001 to $50,000,000 $1,000,000 $50,000,001 to $75,000,000 $1,500,000 $75,000,001 to $100,000,000 $2,000,000 Greater than $100,000,000 $2,500,000
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'net sales' to *include* returns, directly contradicting the contradicted text and the original definition within the same clause, which explicitly states 'net sales' is defined as 'gross sales *minus* returns'. This creates a direct conflict in the royalty calculation, as the basis for the royalty payment is altered, leading to a higher 'net sales' figure and therefore a higher royalty payment than initially agreed upon. This creates a structural flaw as it leads to in-text contradiction, violating the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: The Services Term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive two-year terms thereafter unless written notice is given by either party to the other, indicating that party's intention not to renew the Services Term of this Agreement, at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the initial Services Term or any renewed Services Term.
perturbation.changed_text: The Services Term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive two-year terms thereafter unless written notice is given by Dr. Murray, indicating that party's intention not to renew the Services Term of this Agreement, at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the initial Services Term or any renewed Services Term.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting the ability to provide notice to only Dr. Murray and changing the timeline to 30 days, a contradiction is created. Previously, either party could prevent auto-renewal with 90 days notice. Now, EHS and EHN are bound by the auto-renewal unless Dr. Murray acts. This contrasts with the initial intention for mutual control over the agreement's term described elsewhere, specifically section 6.2 that alludes to both parties being able to terminate the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination EHS or EHN, on the one hand, and Dr. Murray, on the other, may terminate any Services Term of this Agreement by delivering 60 days written notice to the other party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, EHS or EHN may immediately terminate the Services Term without notice should Dr. Murray be in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text allowed either party to prevent automatic renewal with 90 days' notice. The changed text restricts this ability solely to Dr. Murray and reduces the notice period to 30 days. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that "EHS or EHN, on the one hand, and Dr. Murray, on the other, may terminate any Services Term of this Agreement by delivering 60 days written notice to the other party." The changed text now requires Dr. Murray to act to prevent auto-renewal, stripping EHS and EHN of their previously granted ability to terminate. Furthermore, the change from 90 to 30 days contradicts the 60-day notice period for termination. This creates a clear conflict and falls under structural flaws.

perturbation.original_text: Dr. Murray has unique qualifications to provide the services contemplated herein, and shall not assign any of its or his rights or obligations to any other person or entity without EHS's written consent, which may be withheld or granted in EHS's discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Dr. Murray has unique qualifications to provide the services contemplated herein, and shall assign any of its or his rights or obligations to any other person or entity without EHS's written consent, which may be withheld or granted in Dr. Murray's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not assign' to 'shall assign' directly contradicts the initial restriction on assignment. Moreover, assigning the consent discretion to Dr. Murray makes the opposite of the initial sentence, which was to give EHS the final word. This alteration creates uncertainty because it is unclear whether Dr. Murray has to approve an assignation of his own obligations. This also creates tension with Article 9(4), which underlines the lack of agent association, thus contradicting the concept of assignation for duties between the signing parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consent Required: Neither party is the agent or franchisee of the other party, and neither party, under any circumstances, may bind the other party to any agreement or obligation to any third person without the written consent of the party being bound. In all matters relating to thisAgreement, both parties shall be acting solely as independent contractors and shall be solely responsible for the acts of their respective employees, contractors and agents. Employees, agents and contractors of one party shall not be considered employees, agents or contractors of the other party. Any consent or approval by a party to this Agreement shall be made only by a duly authorized officer of that party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall not assign' to 'shall assign' directly contradicts the initial restriction on assignment, and assigning consent discretion to Dr. Murray contradicts the original intention of giving EHS the final word. This alteration creates uncertainty and directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which specifies that neither party is the agent of the other and cannot bind the other to any agreement without written consent. By forcing assignment and giving Dr. Murray control over the assignment, it potentially binds EHS to an agreement against its will, creating a clear contradiction.
